senses.definition: to convey one's admiration or approval for someone

examples.x: It's always nice to pay a compliment to someone when you notice their efforts.
It's polite to pay a compliment when you appreciate someone's outfit or appearance.

senses.definition: to settle an obligation by returning borrowed money or fulfilling a financial commitment

examples.x: She used her savings to pay a debt that had been weighing on her for months.
Paying a debt on time can help maintain a good credit score.

senses.definition: to provide a specified amount of money as a penalty for breaking a rule, law, or regulation

examples.x: He had to pay a fine for parking his car in a no-parking zone.
The company paid a fine for environmental violations.
She received a citation for littering and had to pay a fine for the offense.
They paid a fine for violating the noise ordinance last night.

senses.definition: to concentrate or focus on a particular task or subject

examples.x: Please pay attention during the lecture to understand the material.
Parents often encourage their children to pay attention in school to learn and succeed.
The teacher requested that the students pay attention during the test.

word_list.pronunciation: pˈeɪ ɐtˈɛnʃən tʊ ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to carefully watch, consider, or listen to someone or something

examples.x: Please pay attention to the safety instructions before we begin.
Make sure to pay attention to the tour guide so you don't get lost in the museum.
It's important to pay attention to the details when working on this project.
Please pay attention to your mother when she's giving you instructions.
She asked her students to pay attention to the main themes in the novel.

word_list.pronunciation: pˈeɪ tɹˈɪbjuːt tʊ ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to show respect, admiration, or recognition to someone or something

examples.x: The community gathered to pay tribute to the local hero with a heartfelt ceremony.
At the award ceremony, they paid tribute to the achievements of outstanding individuals.
They gathered to pay tribute to the fallen soldiers on Memorial Day.
The artist's work pays tribute to the beauty of nature and the environment.
The museum's exhibit pays tribute to the cultural heritage of the region.

senses.definition: to go to a specific location, typically for social, friendly, or personal reasons

examples.x: We decided to pay a visit to our grandparents over the weekend.
They paid a visit to the local animal shelter to adopt a new pet.
Friends often pay a visit to catch up, share stories, and have a good time.
After a long absence, she thought it was time to pay a visit to her childhood neighborhood.

senses.definition: to show honor and admiration to someone or something, typically in response to a loss, a situation, or in acknowledgment of someone's significance

examples.x: Paying respect to one's elders is a traditional value in many cultures.
The soldiers were honored for their bravery in a ceremony where people paid respect.

senses.definition: to show honor, reverence, or farewell to a deceased person, typically by attending their funeral, memorial service, or viewing the body before burial or cremation

examples.x: The entire community came together to pay their last respects to the firefighter who had served with distinction.
At the funeral, attendees had the opportunity to pay their last respects and say their final goodbyes.
Yesterday, I paid my last respects to a dear friend at the memorial service.
They are currently paying their last respects at the open casket.
He traveled from across the country to pay his last respects to his late mentor.

senses.definition: to give attention or serious consideration to a specific matter, advice, or warning

examples.x: If you want to be successful in your career, it's important to pay heed to your mentor's advice.
Parents should pay heed to their children's concerns and address them in a timely manner.
Paying heed to the traffic rules can help prevent accidents and ensure safety on the road.
When hiking in the wilderness, it is crucial to pay heed to the weather forecast and plan accordingly.
It's important to pay heed to your body's signals and get enough rest when you're feeling tired.

senses.definition: to show deep respect, honor, or reverence to someone or something, often in acknowledgment of their significance, influence, or achievements

examples.x: The annual festival pays homage to the culture and traditions of the region.
Visitors pay homage at the graves of historical figures to honor their contributions.
People from around the world gathered to pay homage to the great humanitarian leader.
The ceremony paid homage through various rituals and customs.
The film pays homage to classic movies of the 1950s through its storytelling and cinematography.

senses.definition: to produce a profitable return or benefit as a result of an investment of time, effort, or resources

examples.x: Our efforts are finally paying dividends.
The time he spent learning English paid dividends when he started looking for a job.
Regular exercise and a healthy diet pay dividends in terms of improved health and well-being.
Investing in quality education pays dividends throughout one's life.
Implementing sustainable practices in business operations pays dividends by reducing costs and enhancing reputation.
